LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT DIGNITY HEALTH DBA ST. MARY'S MEDICAL CENTER DEMURRER TO AMENDED COMPLAINT. OFF CALENDAR. THE BANE DECLARATION FAILS TO COMPLY WITH CCP 430.41(A)(3)(A) AND DOES NOT EXPRESSLY SHOW THE "MEANS" BY WHICH THE PARTIES MET AND CONFERRED (BY TELEPHONE OR IN PERSON). THE PARTIES ARE ORDERED TO COMPLY WITH THE CODE. DIGNITY HEALTH'S RESPONSE TO THE AMENDED COMPLAINT IS NOW DUE BY DECEMBER 8, 2023. JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER JR., CLERK: M. GOODMAN, NOT REPORTED. (302/RBU)

MASTER CALENDAR JURY

MOTION TO ADMIT COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE
Off Calendar Jun-02-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

1 - Defendant Karl Martin Meisel, MD's unopposed Motion to Admit Pro Hac Vice Counsel Thomas Andrew Graham is GRANTED.

Good cause appearing, counsel Thomas Andrew Graham may appear as counsel pro hac vice for Moving Party in this action.

Moving Party is ordered to prepare a proposed order which repeats the above text verbatim and email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the time set for hearing.

Order is signed.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

MOTION TO ADMIT COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE
Off Calendar Jun-02-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

2 - Defendant Karl Martin Meisel, MD's unopposed Motion to Admit Pro Hac Vice Counsel John E. Hall is OFF CALENDAR.

Pursuant to Moving Party's 5/26/2026 Notice of Withdrawal, this motion is ordered off calendar.

Moving Party is ordered to prepare a proposed order which repeats the above text verbatim and email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the time set for hearing.

No proposed order received. Moving party failed to provide and follow the court's order.

Counsel for the moving party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the court's ruling and must drop a hard courtesy copy in Dept. 302's inbox.
Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

MOTION TO ADMIT COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE
Off Calendar May-01-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
David Depolo, Esq. 925-287-8181 Donnelly Nelson & Depolo APC for Defendant.
No other Appearance.

Party states appearance. The Court announces the tentative ruling. The Court states a timely notice of contest the TR was received. The Court adds context and admonishes Counsel on technicality and submission of proposed orders prio to the hearing.

ARGUED.

Mr. Depolo apologizes profusely to the Court, no intent to jump the line, inadvertently missed 2 days for e-service, legal assistant is new and ill, did not double check, save the court some additional time.

The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

The Court adopts the tentative ruling.

1-DEFENDANT KARL MARTIN MEISEL, M.D. MOTION TO ADMIT COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE.

Defendant Karl Martin Meisel, M.D.'s unopposed Motion to Admit Counsel John E. Hall, Jr. Pro Hac Vice is DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE.

Moving Defendant has not demonstrated he timely served his motion. (See Code of Civil Procedure section 1005(b) [motion must be filed and served 16 court days before hearing]; Code of Civil Procedure section 1013(a) [service by mail where place of address and place of mailing are within State of adds 5 calendar days to the period of notice]; see Code of Civil Procedure section 1010.6(a)(3)(B) [service by email adds two court days to "any period of notice"]; Code of Civil Procedure section 1013(e) [service by fax only be agreement and adds two court days to "any period of notice"].) Moving Defendant electronically served his motion on 4/9/2026, which is not 16 court days plus two court days before 5/1//2026, the hearing date noticed. Defendant's disregard for the rules and attempt to jump the line is not excused.

Moving Party must prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the above and email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the time set for hearing.

Order is signed.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-09-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT DIGNITY HEALTH DBA ST. MARY'S MEDICAL CENTER DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT.

Off calendar. The Bane declaration fails to comply with CCP 430.41(a)(3)(A) and does not expressly show the "means" by which the parties met and conferred (by telephone or in person). The parties are ordered to comply with the code. Dignity Health's response to the amended complaint is now due by December 8, 2023.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/RBU)
